twistCard: The thread goes viral by 9am. The replies are split into two camps: the ones who already hate you, and the ones who are about to.

scenes.title: Scene 5 · The Hater Tweet
scenes.imagePrompt: rooftop bar in SoMa at golden hour, a male competing-startup CEO in expensive streetwear leaning on a railing, phone held up showing a tweet, founder's silhouette half-turned, smug condescension, two-shot

dialogue.speaker: hater
dialogue.text: Hey. Big fan. Just posted a thread about Wagr — my views are my own, obviously.

dialogue.speaker: hater
dialogue.text: Saw the term sheet news. Bold of you. Gutsy. Thirteen percent dilution at three mil though, brutal.

dialogue.speaker: hater
dialogue.text: Anyway, my Series B closed at four-fifty post. Just so you know what real numbers look like in this market.

dialogue.speaker: hater
dialogue.text: Want to grab coffee? I can intro you to my partners. They love a comeback story.

choices.label: Block him publicly
choices.consequence: Founder retaliates; the hater's quote-tweet army doubles overnight.

choices.label: Take the meeting
choices.consequence: Founder humors him; the meeting was a recruiting trap.

choices.label: Ignore. Eat the dunk.
choices.consequence: Founder absorbs the hit; the timeline moves on by lunch.

scenes.title: Scene 6 · The YC Office Hours
scenes.imagePrompt: a YC partner's small Palo Alto office, an older woman with reading glasses pushed up, warm but searching expression, founder seated across a wooden desk strewn with batch-house mugs and one rejection letter, two-shot, late-afternoon golden light

dialogue.speaker: mentor
dialogue.text: I've watched two hundred startups die. The dead ones almost always look like yours did three weeks ago.

dialogue.speaker: mentor
dialogue.text: Here's what I want you to consider. Not as advice. As a question.

dialogue.speaker: mentor
dialogue.text: If you fired Maya tomorrow, would the company still ship Q3? Would it still be Wagr?

choices.label: Yes. I am Wagr.
choices.consequence: Founder claims the company; the mentor stops returning calls.

choices.label: No. We are.
choices.consequence: Founder credits the team; the mentor opens her contact list.

choices.label: I don't know yet.
choices.consequence: Founder hesitates; the mentor writes the answer in her notebook.

scenes.title: Scene 7 · The Pre-Empt
scenes.imagePrompt: the Battery members-only club, exposed brick and $24 cocktails, a patrician VC across a small leather banquette, founder's drink untouched, leaning in for the close, intimate two-shot, late evening

dialogue.speaker: vc
dialogue.text: The other firm's term sheet is a trap and you know it. We can pre-empt at two-X the valuation tonight.

dialogue.speaker: vc
dialogue.text: But I need a yes before the bill comes. Tomorrow this conversation gets re-priced.

dialogue.speaker: vc
dialogue.text: And I need you to commit to the AI pivot publicly. Tweet it tomorrow morning. Tag us.

choices.label: Sign tonight
choices.consequence: Founder takes the bigger check; the public pivot tweet goes live at 7am.

choices.label: Need 48 hours
choices.consequence: Founder asks for time; the offer evaporates.

scenes.title: Scene 8 · TechCrunch Calls Back
scenes.imagePrompt: a Mission cafe at noon, third-wave espresso machine in background, the male reporter from before now seated alone, phone face-up between you, his recorder visibly running, founder's hands tense on the table, two-shot

dialogue.speaker: reporter
dialogue.text: We're publishing in three hours. The headline currently reads 'Wagr Founder Pushed Out CTO For VC Money'.

dialogue.speaker: reporter
dialogue.text: I'm giving you one chance to give me a quote. Anything. The lawyers say the headline stands either way.

dialogue.speaker: reporter
dialogue.text: Or — if you want to make news instead of being it — I have a different angle. Off the record.

choices.label: Give him a quote
choices.consequence: Founder feeds the headline; it shifts from 'pushed out' to 'in transition'.

choices.label: Take the off-record
choices.consequence: Founder trades a different scoop; a competitor's funding round leaks instead.

choices.label: Hang up
choices.consequence: Founder cuts contact; the headline runs as written.
